page: Amazon Web Services m7a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, VNNI, and BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the m7a.xlarge demonstrates strong single-core performance, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Geekbench single-core evaluations. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing also rank in the top tier. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. While small-block cached memory read bandwidth is average at 585.94 GB/sec, large-block uncached read bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 32.75 GB/sec. Other operations, such as Redis SET and static web serving, maintain average performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7a.xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads that require strong single-thread execution without massive memory or local storage demands. The inclusion of DDR5 memory and dedicated AMD EPYC cores provides a modern hardware foundation without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or storage-optimized instances. This instance is well-suited for applications such as web serving, microservices, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and provides a baseline network speed of 2 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, alongside average multi-core and database execution capabilities, though uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is a relative bottleneck. This resource profile makes the m7a.xlarge highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, microservices, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring a dedicated 4-core AMD EPYC processor, 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with a 4-core AMD EPYC CPU, 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, high-speed DDR5 memory, and strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 4 physical cores at 3.7 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 4.0 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or bundled GPU resources
Suited for web serving, microservices, and software compilation
